notes.purpose: Canonical jewellery taxonomy for scraping-time classification and Shopify seeding. See CATEGORISATION.md for definitions and research sources.
notes.matching: 1) case- and diacritic-insensitive exact match against label+synonyms; 2) embedding similarity using embedding_text = '<label>. Synonyms: <synonyms>. <definition>'; 3) below threshold -> unclassified, log the source term.

terms.label: Ring
terms.definition: Jewellery worn on the finger.

terms.label: Necklace
terms.definition: Jewellery worn around the neck.

terms.label: Earring
terms.definition: Jewellery worn on the ear.

terms.label: Bracelet
terms.definition: Jewellery worn around the wrist.

terms.label: Chain
terms.definition: Necklace whose interest is the metal links themselves; worn alone or as a layering base.

terms.label: Choker
terms.definition: Short necklace sitting tight at the base of the neck, roughly 35-40cm.

terms.label: Pendant
terms.definition: Chain carrying a single focal drop such as a coin, medallion, amulet, cross or initial.

terms.label: Locket
terms.definition: Pendant that opens to hold a photo or keepsake.

terms.label: Lariat
terms.definition: Open-ended necklace that drapes or pulls through itself in a Y shape.

terms.label: Station
terms.definition: Chain with gems or motifs spaced at intervals along its length.

terms.label: Tennis Necklace
terms.definition: Continuous line of uniformly set stones around the whole length.

terms.label: Beaded Necklace
terms.definition: Necklace of strung beads or pearls rather than metal links.

terms.label: T-Bar
terms.definition: Chain fastened or decorated with a horizontal bar toggle at the front.

terms.label: Band
terms.definition: Continuous metal band without a focal stone, including wide cigar bands.

terms.label: Signet
terms.definition: Flat-faced ring, traditionally engravable.

terms.label: Stacking
terms.definition: Slim ring designed to be worn in combination with others.

terms.label: Eternity
terms.definition: Stones set uniformly all or half the way around the band.

terms.label: Solitaire
terms.definition: Single focal stone on a plain band.

terms.label: Halo
terms.definition: Centre stone encircled by smaller pave stones.

terms.label: Three Stone
terms.definition: Three or paired stones set in a row.

terms.label: Cluster
terms.definition: Multiple small stones grouped into one motif.

terms.label: Cocktail
terms.definition: Oversized decorative ring with a dominant stone or motif.

terms.label: Dome
terms.definition: Rounded, puffed profile where the metal shape is the focus.

terms.label: Chain Ring
terms.definition: Flexible ring made of chain links.

terms.label: Open Ring
terms.definition: Band with a deliberate gap or wrap, often adjustable.

terms.label: Pinky
terms.definition: Small-sized ring styled for the little finger.

terms.label: Stud
terms.definition: Earring that sits directly on the lobe on a post.

terms.label: Hoop
terms.definition: Closed circular earring, from small to oversized.

terms.label: Huggie
terms.definition: Small hinged hoop that hugs the lobe.

terms.label: Drop
terms.definition: Earring that hangs below the lobe with movement.

terms.label: Chandelier
terms.definition: Multi-tiered, elaborate drop earring.

terms.label: Ear Cuff
terms.definition: Clamps onto the ear, no piercing needed.

terms.label: Climber
terms.definition: Earring that runs up the ear line from the lobe.

terms.label: Threader
terms.definition: Fine chain pulled through the piercing.

terms.label: Chain Bracelet
terms.definition: Linked-metal bracelet.

terms.label: Bangle
terms.definition: Rigid closed circle that slips over the hand.

terms.label: Cuff
terms.definition: Rigid bracelet with an opening at the back.

terms.label: Tennis Bracelet
terms.definition: Continuous line of uniformly set stones.

terms.label: Charm Bracelet
terms.definition: Bracelet carrying one or more hanging charms.

terms.label: Beaded Bracelet
terms.definition: Bracelet of strung beads or pearls.

terms.label: Cord
terms.definition: Fabric or leather cord bracelet, often with a metal motif.

terms.label: Chunky
terms.definition: Visually heavy, wide or thick proportions.

terms.label: Dainty
terms.definition: Small-scale and fine in proportion.

terms.label: Minimalist
terms.definition: Clean, unadorned design about restraint rather than size.

terms.label: Classic
terms.definition: Conventional forms that do not date, such as plain bands, solitaires and pearls.

terms.label: Modern
terms.definition: Contemporary, design-led, architectural aesthetic.

terms.label: Vintage
terms.definition: Antique-inspired detailing such as milgrain, filigree and old cuts.

terms.label: Organic
terms.definition: Fluid, irregular, molten or nature-derived forms.

terms.label: Textured
terms.definition: Visible surface work such as hammering, ridges or twists.

terms.label: Geometric
terms.definition: Angular shapes as the central motif.

terms.label: Celestial
terms.definition: Star, moon, sun and constellation motifs.

terms.label: Romantic
terms.definition: Hearts, bows, ribbons and love symbolism.

terms.label: Talisman
terms.definition: Symbolic or protective motifs such as evil eyes, amulets, zodiac and chakra.

terms.label: Personalised
terms.definition: Made individual through initials, engraving, names or birthstones.

terms.label: Everyday
terms.definition: Low-key staples meant for constant wear.

terms.label: Boho
terms.definition: Relaxed, beachy, festival-leaning aesthetic with shells, cords and mixed beads.

terms.label: Solid Gold
terms.definition: Jewellery made entirely of gold alloy; purity captured separately.

terms.label: Gold Vermeil
terms.definition: Thick layer of gold over a sterling silver base.

terms.label: Gold Plated
terms.definition: Layer of gold over a brass or steel base.

terms.label: Sterling Silver
terms.definition: 92.5% silver alloy.

terms.label: Platinum
terms.definition: Naturally white precious metal used in fine and bridal jewellery.

terms.label: Stainless Steel
terms.definition: Tarnish-resistant steel or titanium base metal.

terms.label: Mixed Metal
terms.definition: Combination of two or more metal colours or materials.

terms.label: Yellow Gold
terms.definition: Warm yellow gold tone.

terms.label: White Gold
terms.definition: Silvery-white gold alloy tone.

terms.label: Rose Gold
terms.definition: Pink-toned gold alloy.

terms.label: Silver
terms.definition: Silver metal tone.

terms.label: Two-Tone
terms.definition: Two metal tones combined in one piece.

terms.label: 18ct
terms.definition: 75% gold.

terms.label: 14ct
terms.definition: 58.5% gold.

terms.label: 9ct
terms.definition: 37.5% gold.

terms.label: Sterling Silver
terms.definition: 92.5% silver.

terms.label: Diamond
terms.definition: Natural diamond.

terms.label: Lab-Grown Diamond
terms.definition: Laboratory-created diamond.

terms.label: Moissanite
terms.definition: Brilliant lab-created silicon carbide stone.

terms.label: Cubic Zirconia
terms.definition: Affordable lab-created diamond simulant.

terms.label: Pearl
terms.definition: Natural or cultured pearl.

terms.label: Sapphire
terms.definition: Corundum gemstone, classically blue.

terms.label: Emerald
terms.definition: Green beryl gemstone.

terms.label: Ruby
terms.definition: Red corundum gemstone.

terms.label: Amethyst
terms.definition: Purple quartz.

terms.label: Aquamarine
terms.definition: Pale blue beryl.

terms.label: Topaz
terms.definition: Topaz gemstone, commonly blue or white.

terms.label: Citrine
terms.definition: Yellow quartz.

terms.label: Garnet
terms.definition: Deep red gemstone.

terms.label: Peridot
terms.definition: Yellow-green gemstone.

terms.label: Opal
terms.definition: Iridescent gemstone.

terms.label: Moonstone
terms.definition: Feldspar with a milky adularescence.

terms.label: Turquoise
terms.definition: Opaque blue-green stone.

terms.label: Malachite
terms.definition: Banded green stone.

terms.label: Lapis Lazuli
terms.definition: Deep blue stone with gold flecks.

terms.label: Mother of Pearl
terms.definition: Iridescent shell lining.

terms.label: Onyx
terms.definition: Black opaque stone.

terms.label: Enamel
terms.definition: Coloured surface accent merchandised like a stone colour.

terms.label: Round
terms.definition: Circular brilliant cut.

terms.label: Oval
terms.definition: Elongated rounded cut.

terms.label: Pear
terms.definition: Teardrop-shaped cut.

terms.label: Marquise
terms.definition: Pointed-ends elongated cut.

terms.label: Emerald Cut
terms.definition: Rectangular step cut with cropped corners.

terms.label: Cushion
terms.definition: Square-to-rectangular cut with rounded corners.

terms.label: Princess
terms.definition: Square brilliant cut.

terms.label: Radiant
terms.definition: Rectangular brilliant cut with trimmed corners.

terms.label: Asscher
terms.definition: Square step cut.

terms.label: Heart
terms.definition: Heart-shaped cut.

terms.label: Baguette
terms.definition: Slim rectangular step cut, usually an accent stone.

terms.label: Trillion
terms.definition: Triangular cut.

terms.label: Rose Cut
terms.definition: Flat-backed antique cut with a domed, faceted top.

terms.label: Cabochon
terms.definition: Polished, unfaceted domed stone.

terms.label: Briolette
terms.definition: Faceted teardrop drilled for hanging.

terms.label: Claw
terms.definition: Stone held by metal claws; UK term for prong setting.

terms.label: Bezel
terms.definition: Metal rim encircling the stone.

terms.label: Pavé
terms.definition: Surface paved with many tiny stones.

terms.label: Channel
terms.definition: Stones set in a row between two metal walls.

terms.label: Flush
terms.definition: Stone sunk level with the metal surface.

terms.label: Tension
terms.definition: Stone held by pressure so it appears to float.

terms.label: Curb
terms.definition: Flat interlocking twisted links.

terms.label: Cable
terms.definition: Simple uniform oval links; the classic fine chain.

terms.label: Belcher
terms.definition: Round wide links of even thickness.

terms.label: Box
terms.definition: Square cube-shaped links.

terms.label: Rope
terms.definition: Links twisted in a spiral rope pattern.

terms.label: Figaro
terms.definition: Pattern of short links alternating with a long link.

terms.label: Herringbone
terms.definition: Flat, liquid-looking slanted parallel links.

terms.label: Snake
terms.definition: Smooth round chain of tightly joined rings.

terms.label: Paperclip
terms.definition: Elongated flat rectangular links.

terms.label: Ball
terms.definition: Chain of small joined spheres.

terms.label: Byzantine
terms.definition: Intricate interwoven link pattern.
